title: From cloning to gene-editing: the enduring legacy of Dolly the sheep
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02096-1

Dolly’s brush with celebrity could hold lessons for current discussions about reproductive technologies.

title: Universities are relying on AI-detection software to catch cheating. How well do the programs work?
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01358-2

Technology companies say that their services can sniff out AI-generated writing, but their tools vary in technique and quality.

title: Deep-sea oddities and boatloads of other new species — June’s best science images
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02087-2

The month’s sharpest science shots — selected by Nature’s photo team.

title: The rise of computer chips — and the race to control them
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02099-y

An analysis sets out the history of computing beyond software and Californian entrepreneurs.

title: Time to give hydration breaks the red card? What science says about keeping cool
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02097-0

Inappropriate use of cooling breaks in sport is undermining trust in heat-health research.

title: Daily briefing: How cooperation built the world
content:
Nature, Published online: 03 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02116-0

Collaboration in nature has often been overlooked in favour of competition, argues science journalist Rowan Hooper. Plus, what’s really happening with trust in science.

title: Temporal potential-induced regulation for extending alkaline electrolyzer lifespan under power fluctuations
content: Xia et al. report the temporal potential-induced regulation for large-scale alkaline water electrolyzers. Without modifying the catalyst and electrolyzer structures, this approach extends AWE durability via power supply optimization, providing a scalable and effective pathway. This work bridges electrical engineering and electrochemistry, advancing the integration of renewable energy into hydrogen infrastructure.

title: Electrical double-layer dynamics regulation for maximized electrostatic energy harvesting of liquid-solid triboelectric nanogenerators
content: Water-solid friction produces clean and durable electricity upon environmental excitation. By revealing and overcoming the fundamental limitations of liquid-solid triboelectricity, a switch-enhanced generator achieves high energy output, good load adaptability, and can harvest energy from multiple scenarios to power electronics.

title: Engineered living materials need engineered EU regulation
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02651-1

Kai Purnhagen and Justus Wesseler discuss how EU law needs reform to address market access bottlenecks for living composites.

title: Twist-angle-controlled anomalous gating in bilayer graphene/BN heterostructures
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02667-7

Rotational misalignment between the top and bottom boron nitride layers encapsulating graphene acts as a switch for anomalous gating effects, such as gate ineffectiveness and hysteresis. These effects are observed at room temperature within an angle range of 15–45° and are highly sensitive to small angular changes.

title: Multimodal scanning-probe quantum sensing of quantum materials
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02648-w

Solid-state spin defects are a powerful and versatile class of quantum sensor. This Review covers the development of scanning-probe nitrogen-vacancy sensing as a tool for investigating quantum materials with nanoscale resolution, surveys the recent development of spin defects in 1D and 2D host materials and identifies key directions for future research.

title: Thermally activated proton transfer enables air-processed perovskite solar cells approaching 27%
content: Making high-performance perovskite solar cells in ordinary air is difficult because moisture and heat disrupt film formation. Here, a small molecular additive guides crystal growth at the start and then stabilizes the material during annealing, enabling highly efficient devices made outside a glovebox. The work shows that additive design can control both processing and stability at once, offering a practical path toward lower-cost, scalable perovskite solar manufacturing.

title: In situ optical techniques for perovskite crystallization
content: In situ optical characterization techniques have emerged as powerful tools for probing nucleation, phase evolution, and intermediate-state formation in real time. This review provides a systematic overview of the development and application of in situ optical techniques for studying perovskite crystallization. It aims to offer a comprehensive technical guide for researchers seeking to employ in situ optical methods to investigate perovskite crystallization.

title: Switching from insertion to conversion for multielectron aqueous vanadium batteries
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02666-8

Conventional aqueous vanadium-ion batteries have low capacities, as the fundamental charge-storage mechanism is a one-electron insertion process. Here, using a mesoporous carbon shell approach, a four-electron conversion reaction for charge storage is demonstrated.

title: From screening to generative design in nucleic acid delivery
content:
Nature Reviews Materials, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41578-026-00944-0

The discovery of delivery materials is shifting from empirical screening to predictive, artificial intelligence-guided design. Harder problems, including the simultaneous optimization of competing properties and delivery beyond the liver, are now pushing the field further, from screening materials to generating them against predefined property targets.

title: Understanding and engineering electrocatalyst durability
content:
Nature Reviews Materials, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41578-026-00937-z

Electrocatalyst durability is a central challenge limiting the large-scale deployment of electrochemical energy technologies, despite major advances in nanocatalyst design and activity. This Review examines the thermodynamic and kinetic principles governing catalyst stability and highlights emerging strategies to understand and engineer stable, high-performance electrocatalysts for practical energy applications.

title: Found: the toxic killer that caused an ocean catastrophe
content:
Nature, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02112-4

Scientists identify an alga species as the culprit behind the deaths of more than one million marine animals.

title: Pair of ‘super-puff’ planets are lighter than candy floss
content:
Nature, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02114-2

Two gas giants circling the same star are among the least dense planets ever found.

title: Graduating without a thesis: meet the people getting ‘practical’ PhDs in China
content:
Nature, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01242-z

Nature spoke to three students from China’s first cohort of PhD candidates who are allowed to graduate with products instead of papers.

title: Whoops! Most arXiv papers contain information never meant to be shared
content:
Nature, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02057-8

Passwords, GPS coordinates and private conversations have all been found in the metadata and source files of millions of preprint submissions.

title: ‘This time, it’s the other way around’: how Indonesia is reclaiming the science of human history
content:
Nature, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01357-3

After a massive restructuring of its research ecosystem, Indonesia is taking the lead on its own palaeontological research.

title: Nukes in space? Orbital detector could sniff out warheads
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02141-z

A shoebox-sized detector satellite could identify the telltale signal of a hidden nuclear device — plus, how Indigenous plant knowledge is under threat.

title: Observation of Floquet rotational super-radiance
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10725-y

A Floquet regime of rotational super-radiance has been observed in a spatiotemporally modulated ring network of resonators.

title: In vivo feasibility study of humanoid robots in surgery
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10796-x

A systematic evaluation shows that contemporary humanoid robots can perform laparoscopic surgical tasks through teleoperation, highlighting both their promise and key technical challenges before clinical deployment.

title: Single-phase gradient-solvation-electrolyte-stabilized Li metal batteries
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10732-z

A targeted ligand anti-solvent is incorporated into an anion-rich ether-based electrolyte and used to achieve lithium metal batteries with a long cycle life, high energy density and high capacity retention.

title: Non-genotoxic transplantation and in vivo selection through epitope editing
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10737-8

Epitope editing of KIT enables antibody-based, non-genotoxic conditioning that selectively enriches therapeutic BCL11A-edited haematopoietic stem/progenitor cells, supports durable engraftment, preserves clonal diversity and enhances induction of fetal haemoglobin, a therapeutic approach for conditions such as sickle cell disease and β-thalassemia.

title: Architecture of the 8 MDa Hdr–Vhu–Fwd super-assembly in class I methanogens
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10744-9

The structure of the Hdr–Vhu–Fwd super-assembly reveals a lineage-specific and modular 8 MDa complex with the ability to adapt to diverse anaerobic niches.

title: Reinforcement learning control of quantum error correction
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10759-2

By integrating reinforcement learning with quantum error correction, a quantum computer continuously self-calibrates during computation, achieving record logical error rates and enhanced resilience to drift.

title: Large language models can predict the results of social science experiments
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10742-x

Large language models can be used to estimate the results of social science experiments about as accurately as a group of human forecasters—even for experiments published after the models were trained—although they tended to overestimate effect sizes.

title: Ancient feeding-related neuropeptides regulate alloparenting in ants
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10747-6

Pharmacological screening and behavioural assays in ants identify two specific neuropeptides that have key and opposite effects in regulating brood care, and that link nutritional state to age-dependent changes in parenting behaviour.

title: Anatomy of a seafloor spreading event captured by in situ seismogeodesy
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10785-0

By combining hydroacoustic, direct-path ranging and bottom-pressure measurements, in situ observations of a rifting event at a segment of the Southeast Indian Ridge are reported, providing insight into seafloor spreading events on yearly timescales.

title: Computational approaches and the future of urban crime research
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10622-4

This Review examines the opportunities and challenges introduced by new datasets and computational techniques in urban crime research, and outlines future directions for leveraging these advances effectively.

title: Reconfigurable mmWave microchips co-integrating hBN switches on GaN
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10761-8

Radio-frequency switches made from two-dimensional layered hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) were co-integrated on gallium nitride (GaN) microchips to build programmable millimetre-wave (mmWave) monolithic microwave integrated circuits suitable for 6G hardware.

title: Air-permeable hydrogels through viscoelastic phase separation of aerogels
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10712-3

Viscoelastic phase separation is used to fabricate non-collapsible, air-rich networks in high-water-content hydrogels containing silica aerogel beads, allowing air to permeate through the material and enabling a tenfold increase in oxygen permeability over pristine hydrogels.

title: Verification of the Outer Space Treaty with cosmic protons
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10783-2

By observing the neutrons induced by spallation from protons in the inner Van Allen radiation belts using widely available sensor technologies, the detection of a thermonuclear weapon carried by a space satellite is possible.

title: Aneuploidy selects for the acquisition of driver genes in breast cancer
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10752-9

Screening of chromosome arm-level aneuploidies uncovers that these alterations select for specific basal-like breast cancer driver genes, including PLGRKT, an oncogene whose tumour-promoting activity is associated with highly stress-resistant mitochondria and enhanced detoxification of reactive oxygen species.

title: Universal cell embedding provides a foundation model for cell biology
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10689-z

The universal cell embedding foundation model learns to capture the organization and variation of cells by training on 36 million cells from hundreds of experiments, dozens of tissues and eight species.

title: LARES-2 satellite measures frame-dragging effect around the Earth
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10715-0

Using LARES-2, LAGEOS and GRACE satellites, researchers measured Earth’s frame-dragging with unprecedented precision, strongly confirming general relativity, constraining alternative gravity theories, and improving measurements of Earth’s tides.

title: Chromatin landscape and epigenetic heterogeneity of acute myeloid leukaemia
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10703-4

An ATAC-seq-based approach is used to classify acute myeloid leukaemia (AML) into 16 epigenomic subgroups, and provides insight into the role of non-genetic mechanisms in determining pathogenesis, clinical behaviour and drug sensitivity in this disease.

title: Diet–microbiome synergy underlies obesity-associated immunotherapy efficacy
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10750-x

Diet shapes obesity-associated therapeutic responses to immune checkpoint inhibitors through gut microbial metabolism and host anti-tumour immunity, demonstrated in mouse custom-diet models and human-to-mouse fecal microbiota transplantation experiments.

title: Intergenerational mobility fosters innovation in Europe
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10736-9

The EUROPE-IGM-ATLAS reveals spatiotemporal trends that characterize the changing geography of opportunity in Europe and its relationship with regional innovation.

title: An intrinsic cytoskeletal oscillator establishes neuronal polarity
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10755-6

The ARP2/3 complex works together with actomyosin in an oscillatory program at the soma to govern neuronal polarization.

title: The forest of knowledge under global change
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10741-y

The biocultural heritage of the Amazon, its plant species, plant services and cultures are at risk from climate change.

title: I want to see a prison-to-PhD pipeline
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01673-8

Ryan Rising creates programmes that help people who have been incarcerated to access academic opportunities.

title: Regenerating people–nature relationships to counter biocultural erosion in the Amazon
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01874-1

Rebuilding the reciprocal care-oriented relationships between Amazonian Indigenous peoples and nature could address the predicted effects of climate change on biodiversity.

title: Nobel-winning chemist leaves US to direct AI materials lab in China
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02143-x

Omar Yaghi’s move comes amid cuts to US science and a campaign in China to recruit top international talent.

title: Volcanoes and wildfires contributed to increased stratospheric humidification
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02011-8

Observations from satellites and climate-model simulations show that moderate volcanic eruptions and extreme wildfires have humidified the stratosphere since 2005. The aerosol-mediated effects of these eruptions and fires explain about one-third of the observed stratospheric water-vapour trend, a contribution similar to that of surface warming.

title: Those who forget
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01933-7

Clarity of thought.

title: Ocean floor witnessed splitting apart for the first time — releasing lava
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02139-7

Scientists observed a mid-ocean ridge widen in real time, recording several metres of sea-floor motion and huge lava outflows.

title: Breast cancer driver genes found by screening chromosome aberrations in vivo
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02014-5

Cancer genomes are unstable, often experiencing gains or losses of whole chromosomes or chromosome arms — changes known as aneuploidies. Experiments in mouse models of breast cancer reveal that these instabilities harbour one or two prevalent genes that drive cancer and that bypass the need to accumulate aneuploidies; they also require an intact microenvironment to produce their effects.

title: Judicious use of LLMs could speed up progress in the social sciences
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01875-0

Large language models could advance experiments in the social sciences, but fundamental questions remain about how they should be used to support research.

title: Sea-floor spreading captured by undersea observatory
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01943-5

A sea-floor monitoring experiment observed oceanic crust being created between two tectonic plates — the first in situ measurement of this phenomenon.

title: How to advance revolutionary science: high turnover, high risk and a licence to fail
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02090-7

Kathleen Fisher reveals the recipe for funding innovative science that she brings to the UK Advanced Research and Invention Agency.

title: Nuclear weapons lurking in space could be tracked down by satellites
content:
Nature, Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01944-4

A nuclear weapon orbiting Earth would emit neutrons through its interactions with the planet’s space radiation environment, and these could be detected by satellites.

title: Publisher Correction: A 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum computer with all-to-all connectivity
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10882-0

Publisher Correction: A 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum computer with all-to-all connectivity

title: Listen to Gen Z when it comes to AI in education
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02110-6

Listen to Gen Z when it comes to AI in education

title: Say hello to hard helium
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02101-7

The first solidification of the second-lightest element, and evidence of an ancient salt-water lake in Peru, in this week’s pick from the Nature archive.

title: AI can cause harm: safeguards must catch up
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02109-z

AI can cause harm: safeguards must catch up

title: How long can humans live? All evidence points to a maximum of 125 years
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02111-5

How long can humans live? All evidence points to a maximum of 125 years

title: ‘Humanizer’ tool can erase signs of AI-written text — alarming scientists
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02105-3

Tool directs text-generation systems to remove constructions associated with artificial intelligence in research papers and grant proposals.

title: How to widen access to the critical minerals that the world needs
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02100-8

A patent pool and licensing architecture would enable more widespread use of key minerals than would decades of mining and refining investment alone.

title: Daily briefing: Three decades of Dolly
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02136-w

Look back at the thirty years of advances since the birth of Dolly the sheep. Plus, what the science says about staving off dementia and a call to preserve the Hubble space telescope.

title: Body-plan organizer in comb jellies hints at animal ancestry
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02040-3

Transplantation experiments in evolutionarily distinct animal groups reveal conserved mechanisms underlying tissue organization during development.

title: Save Hubble: the race to preserve the space telescope kicks off
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02000-x

The orbiting observatory’s unique vision and public support continue to be crucial for understanding the Universe.

title: Keep the Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes alive — the science is worth the price tag
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02095-2

These collaborations are delivering scientific discoveries beyond most astronomers’ dreams.

title: How to avoid dementia — what the science really says
content:
Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02098-z

Ambitious studies have been examining the protective effects of diet, exercise and socializing — with surprising results.

title: Daily briefing: World Cup ‘hydration breaks’ miss the mark
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02130-2

The breaks risk undermining trust in heat-health research, argues physiologist Harry Brown. Plus, the month’s best science images and get to grips with computer chips.

title: In situ growth and crystal monitoring for high-efficiency vacuum-deposited perovskite-based solar cells
content: Vacuum deposition offers an industry-compatible route for perovskite solar cells, yet controlling perovskite crystallization remains challenging. Choi et al. introduce a structure-directing layer beneath a perovskite absorber and use in situ photoluminescence to monitor the nucleation, strain, and defects of perovskite, leading to efficient devices with improved stability.

title: Triple-junction all-perovskite solar cells with self-assembling hole contacts in all subcells
content: This study presents a bilayer strategy employing graphene oxide and a self-assembled monolayer for Sn-Pb perovskite solar cells. This approach enables efficient and stable triple-junction all-perovskite solar cells, achieving a 27.3% efficiency alongside exceptional operational stability with a T90 of 770 h.

title: A dilute nitrate electrolyte for all-climate batteries
content: You et al. report a data-driven strategy that enables inexpensive lithium nitrate to serve as the main salt in a dilute lithium-ion battery electrolyte. The designed low-concentration electrolyte reduces cost while stabilizing battery interfaces, enabling all-climate operation in pouch cells.

title: Geopolymer-based electrolytes derived from industrial solid waste for structural energy storage
content: Turning waste into useful infrastructure is central to low-carbon energy storage. This study converts fly ash and slag into a strong, ion-conducting geopolymer electrolyte for zinc-ion brick batteries. By engineering connected pores that guide ions efficiently, the material combines mechanical durability with long-term electrochemical performance, pointing toward building components that can both bear loads and store energy.

title: Household coordination can aid solar adoption
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02103-9

Coordination and active deliberation that includes the whole household can facilitate solar adoption. Interventions targeting energy adoption are likely to be more effective when they are designed to support collective decision making rather than focusing on a single member of the household.

title: Family dynamics are a missing piece in understanding residential solar adoption
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02104-8

Household-wide coordination capacity underpins residential solar adoption. In-sync households share goals, actively deliberate and act collectively to adopt. Oppositional households are misaligned, face coordination snags and do not adopt.

title: Academic battery passports for the regulatory era of energy storage
content: Battery passports are becoming essential for traceability, safety, and circularity in energy storage, yet academic battery research remains largely narrative and difficult to integrate into digital ecosystems. This commentary proposes an academic battery passport framework to enable interoperable, machine-readable battery science for regulation, AI, and life cycle intelligence.

title: Scalable interface-interphase design of tin electrodes enables pouch-type sodium all-solid-state batteries
content: We present an interface-interphase engineering strategy for Sn anodes in sodium all-solid-state batteries, which is compatible with roll-to-roll electrode manufacturing. By integrating an infiltrated sodium closo-borate anolyte with a CNT scaffold, the engineered Sn anode enables reversible alloying chemistry and low-pressure operational capability. Combined with a dry-process fabrication strategy for cathode and solid-electrolyte films, this design enables the first pouch-type NaSSB demonstration.
